guardrail: static projection of the public register; no facts added here

data.what_this_is: The per-jurisdiction signals from which every one of the 132 directed corridor classes is derived by the published rule tools/class_rule.py:class_of. GENERATED — do not edit. The signal VALUES are curated in analysis/signal_table.source.json; scripts/gen_signal_table.py joins them to the register's 152 node records and populates `record_ref`, which closes the corridor-layer face of finding C1 (backlog VB-11).
data.independence_note: This table was NOT reverse-engineered from the shipped class_codes -- the failure mode docs/PROPOSAL_signal_table.md warned about. It was written from the primary-source legal facts and it CONTRADICTS the v0.9.99 artifact on 12 of the 132 edges (8 inbound-US: II -> T, per GENIUS s.20; TW->CN and KR->CN: III -> blocked; CN->KR and TW->KR: III -> pre_regime, per the Atlas's destination-first precedence). Those 12 edges were then corrected in the artifact, not the table. A signal table that agrees with the artifact by construction proves nothing; one that overturns it proves the recompute is real.
data.record_ref_status: POPULATED on 39 of 60 signals, from the register's own node records; 21 carry an explicit, reasoned `record_ref_gap` instead of a fabricated locator (citation firewall, Citable by Construction §4.3). VB-11 is closed for this register: the 152 node records ARE published here, so the locator is supplied rather than declined. A `record_ref` is tagged `match: same_instrument` only where the record rests on the very instrument the signal names; otherwise `related_dimension`, and the difference is emitted under `record_binding_findings` rather than smoothed over.

source_of_record.note: SG4: the curated values are hashed so a silent edit is detectable. The values were written from the primary instruments and CONTRADICT the pre-v0.10.0 corridor artifact on 12 of 132 edges; a signal table that agreed with the artifact by construction would prove nothing.

gates_over_this_file.SIG1: signal-provenance (Citable by Construction s.4.2): every signal the class function READS must be claim_class == tier1_legal. `token_in_issue` is tier2_operational and is never read -- proved by a negative test that flips it and asserts no class moves.
gates_over_this_file.SIG2: binding-status cap (Citable by Construction s.3.1/s.4.2): a signal at evidence_tier == resolution_text must have binding_status == in_force_enacted. This is why the US and UK inbound gates are capped at `mixed` even though their statutory text has been read: an instrument must be IN FORCE, not merely located, to carry the top provenance tier. The cap is what produces the US -> `T` reclassification (VB-06/E1).
gates_over_this_file.SIG3: every tier1_legal signal carries a non-empty `instrument` string.
gates_over_this_file.PR1: tools/recompute_classes.py: class_of(origin, destination) over this table reproduces all 132 class_codes and all as_of_timeline resolves_to values in the artifact, exactly.
gates_over_this_file.SG1: every class-driving signal's record_ref resolves to an existing tier1_legal node record.
gates_over_this_file.SG2: a same_instrument record_ref whose record_binding_status differs from the signal's must declare a divergence reason.
gates_over_this_file.SG4: the curated source's sha256 is stamped, so an unreviewed edit to the values is detectable.

class_rule.name: destination-first precedence (Corridor Atlas v0.2.5, s.3.4 and s.4.1)
class_rule.atlas_quote: Three classes are determined entirely by the destination ... the destination sets the class on every edge except the twenty-seven where an origin-side issuance gap overrides it.
precedence: 1. inbound_gate.value == 'prohibited'  -> blocked      (destination prohibition dominates: Atlas 'every edge terminating in Mainland China is Blocked')
2. inbound_gate.value == 'none'        -> pre_regime   (destination has no authorizable pathway to receive into)
3. origin token_regime.value != 'authorizable' -> III  (origin drag: no exportable, comprehensively authorizable token)
4. inbound_gate.binding_status == 'made_not_commenced' -> T (regime adopted, not yet operative)
5. otherwise the operative gate type: authorization -> I; channel|comparability -> II
class_rule.why_origin_drag_ranks_third: A prohibition and a pre-regime absence are terminal facts about the destination: no origin-side token, present or future, can complete the corridor. Category III means 'the composition is unresolved; partnership or coordination routes remain', which is FALSE where the destination bans the instrument outright. Ranking origin drag above them (as the artifact did through v0.9.99) produced TW->CN and KR->CN reading `III` -- 'partnership route' -- while their own prose read 'Issuance and circulation prohibited'. That is the eighth review's NF-1, in the optimistic direction. Fixed in v0.10.0.
class_rule.origin_drag_reads_a_legal_signal: Origin drag reads `token_regime` (tier1_legal: is there an authorizable issuance regime IN FORCE at the origin?), never `token_in_issue` (tier2_operational: does a circulating token exist?). This is NF-3. The distinction is load-bearing for the US: no federal payment-stablecoin authorization is in force (GENIUS s.20), but state trust / money-transmitter regimes authorize USD payment-stablecoin issuance today, so token_regime == authorizable on a LEGAL ground, not because USDC happens to exist. The same reading keeps the UK a token-holding origin (EMRs 2011 e-money) while its NEW comprehensive regime is made-not-commenced.

record_binding_findings.posture: Disagreement between the signal layer and the node-record layer is surfaced as a finding, never resolved by overwriting either layer (Citable by Construction §5.2). D1 = the two layers disagree on the binding status of the SAME instrument (the load-bearing case is US §18: Act-enacted vs section-operative — errata E1 / VB-06). D2 = the record covers the dimension under a DIFFERENT instrument (US and UK token_regime — VB-09 stays open).

findings.reason: The signal rests on the state trust-company / money-transmitter regimes (e.g. NYDFS 23 NYCRR Part 200) that authorize USD payment-stablecoin issuance today. The register carries no record for those state regimes; `us-pss-issuer_pathway-001` records the FEDERAL GENIUS Act layer, whose §3 issuance prohibition bites only from the §20 effective date. Same dimension, different instrument. Whether a state-authorized issuer is an 'exportable, comprehensively authorizable token' for Atlas purposes is the open question in VB-09; if the answer is no, all 11 outbound-US edges fall to Category III.

`us-pss-monetary_sovereignty-001` cites the GENIUS Act, pinpoint 'Foreign payment stablecoin issuers — comparability determination and registration' — the same §18 gate the signal names. The record codes the ACT as in_force_enacted (it is: enacted 2025-07-18); the signal codes the GATE, which §20 makes effective on the earlier of 2027-01-18 or final-rules + 120 days. Act-enacted is not section-operative. This is errata E1 / VB-06, and it is the divergence that moves the 8 inbound-US edges from II to T.
Same §20 effective-date distinction as US.inbound_gate: the record codes the Act, the signal codes §4(a)(11)'s operative bite.

The signal rests on the Electronic Money Regulations 2011 (SI 2011/99), under which GBP e-money issuance is authorizable today. `uk-frs-issuer_pathway-001` records the NEW comprehensive regime (SI 2026/102), which is made-not-commenced and does not remove the existing pathway. Same dimension, different instrument.

`uk-frs-permitted_activity_yield-001` is coded `pending_proposal` (SI 2026/102 is silent on yield; the Bank of England Nov 2025 materials are a consultation). The signal codes the made-not-commenced regime's restriction. Different instruments within one dimension.

The record bundles the in-force Payment Services Act 2019 baseline with the finalized-policy Single-Currency Stablecoin framework and is coded by the SCS layer (finalized_policy_pending). The signal is coded by the PSA baseline, which is what determines the class. Divergence declared.
Same bundling as SG.token_regime: the class rests on the in-force PSA 2019 baseline, the record is coded by the pending SCS layer.

`sg-scs-distribution-001` carries the PSA 2019 / MAS SCS distribution perimeter. The signal rests on the MAS Digital Token Service Provider statement (June 2025), which the register does not carry as a separate record. Nearest dimension, different instrument.

Vocabulary divergence only: the register's binding_status enum carries `prohibition` as a first-class value; the curated signal writes `in_force_enacted` of the prohibiting instrument (银发〔2026〕42号, in force 2026-02-06). Same instrument, same fact, two encodings.
As CN.token_regime: `prohibition` vs `in_force_enacted` of the prohibiting instrument.
The record carries PIPL / DSL / CAC — the cross-border DATA half of the override. The mainland capital-flow half (SAFE) is not separately recorded. Partial locator, declared as such.
The signal value is `n_a` (a prohibition leaves no yield question to answer). The record rests on the 2017 token-fundraising notice, not on 42号.

the record covers permitted_activity_yield under a different instrument

`tw-frs-monetary_sovereignty-001` still cites the 'draft Virtual Asset Service Act'. The Act was passed at third reading on 2026-06-30 and is enacted, not draft. The signal is correct and the record's source prose is stale — the node-record face of finding F-TW. Recorded here rather than silently corrected: a legal record is edited by a verification pass, not by a build script.

Vocabulary divergence: the register codes the draft Digital Asset Basic Act as `pending_proposal` (there IS a bill); the signal codes the absence of an operative issuance regime as `no_regime` (there is nothing in force to authorize under). Same instruments, different question asked of them.
As KR.token_regime: `pending_proposal` (the bill exists) vs `no_regime` (nothing operative).
`kr-frs-monetary_sovereignty-001` cites the Foreign Exchange Transactions Act — the instrument the conditional, dormant Korea-FX override rests on.

token_regime.instrument: State trust-company / money-transmitter regimes authorizing USD payment-stablecoin issuance (e.g. NYDFS 23 NYCRR Part 200). The federal layer (GENIUS Act, Pub. L. 119-27, enacted 2025-07-18) is NOT yet in force: s.3's issuance prohibition bites only from the s.20 effective date.
token_regime.note: Origin-drag input. Whether a state-authorized issuer counts as an 'exportable, comprehensively authorizable token' for Atlas purposes is the open question in VB-09. If the answer is no, all 11 outbound-US edges fall to III and the I/II structure changes -- the reductio the eighth review's NF-3 raised. Stated here rather than left implicit in a market record.

record_ref.note: The signal rests on the state trust-company / money-transmitter regimes (e.g. NYDFS 23 NYCRR Part 200) that authorize USD payment-stablecoin issuance today. The register carries no record for those state regimes; `us-pss-issuer_pathway-001` records the FEDERAL GENIUS Act layer, whose §3 issuance prohibition bites only from the §20 effective date. Same dimension, different instrument. Whether a state-authorized issuer is an 'exportable, comprehensively authorizable token' for Atlas purposes is the open question in VB-09; if the answer is no, all 11 outbound-US edges fall to Category III.

inbound_gate.instrument: GENIUS Act s.18 (foreign payment-stablecoin issuer comparability determination by the Secretary of the Treasury on SCRC recommendation + OCC registration + US-held reserves), effective per s.20 on the earlier of 18 months after enactment (2027-01-18) or 120 days after the primary federal payment stablecoin regulators issue final regulations.
inbound_gate.evidence_tier_cap_reason: SIG2: the instrument is made_not_commenced, so it cannot carry resolution_text however carefully its text has been read (Citable by Construction s.2.3, s.3.1). Its s.20 effective-date rule IS confirmed against the primary record.
inbound_gate.snapshot_fact: At 2026-06-30 no final implementing regulations had issued: OCC NPRM (published FR 2026-03-02, comments closed 2026-05-01), FDIC NPRM and FinCEN/OFAC joint NPRM (both 2026-04-10), Treasury s.4(c) NPRM (April 2026), NCUA proposal (February 2026). So the 2027-01-18 outer cap binds, and it may fall sooner.
inbound_gate.note: This single signal is the correction that drives E1 / F-US-1 / E8: the s.18 gate is NOT 'available but unused', it is NOT YET AVAILABLE. Coding it as an in-force Category II gate let a market observation ('none granted to date') do a proposition of law's work.

record_ref.note: `us-pss-monetary_sovereignty-001` cites the GENIUS Act, pinpoint 'Foreign payment stablecoin issuers — comparability determination and registration' — the same §18 gate the signal names. The record codes the ACT as in_force_enacted (it is: enacted 2025-07-18); the signal codes the GATE, which §20 makes effective on the earlier of 2027-01-18 or final-rules + 120 days. Act-enacted is not section-operative. This is errata E1 / VB-06, and it is the divergence that moves the 8 inbound-US edges from II to T.
record_ref.divergence: `us-pss-monetary_sovereignty-001` cites the GENIUS Act, pinpoint 'Foreign payment stablecoin issuers — comparability determination and registration' — the same §18 gate the signal names. The record codes the ACT as in_force_enacted (it is: enacted 2025-07-18); the signal codes the GATE, which §20 makes effective on the earlier of 2027-01-18 or final-rules + 120 days. Act-enacted is not section-operative. This is errata E1 / VB-06, and it is the divergence that moves the 8 inbound-US edges from II to T.

inbound_gate.instrument: MiCA authorization wall (Titles III/IV) with the Art. 58(3) means-of-exchange ceiling extending Arts. 22/23/24(3) mutatis mutandis to EMTs denominated in a non-Member-State currency. Art. 23(1) is CONJUNCTIVE: 1,000,000 transactions/day AND EUR 200,000,000/day (quarterly average).
inbound_gate.note: The 'and' (not 'or') is F-EU / VB-07; the papers still write 'or' (errata E2). The EBA operative measurement methodology is pending -- an open parameterization (Feasibility s.3.7), which sharpens the class without moving it.

token_regime.instrument: Payment Services Act Electronic Payment Instruments regime (in force 2023-06-01); closed issuer trichotomy of bank, registered funds-transfer provider, and trust company / trust bank. Act No. 66 of 2025 (promulgated 2025-06-13, in force 2026-06-01) relaxed trust-type reserve composition.

inbound_gate.instrument: PSA Electronic Payment Instruments channel through a registered EPIESP (reserves in Japan; foreign-issuer non-solicitation). Cabinet Office Ordinance promulgated 2026-05-19, in force 2026-06-01, recognises equivalent foreign trust-type stablecoins as electronic payment instruments, subject to (i) an equivalent home licence, (ii) appropriately managed and audited reserves, and (iii) an information-sharing / cooperation arrangement between the home regulator and the JFSA.
inbound_gate.note: Condition (iii) is the hardest gate and the one Feasibility s.5.3 omits (errata F-JP-1); it closes the pathway to jurisdictions with no JFSA MOU and corroborates the s.5.2 bloc structure.

token_regime.instrument: Virtual Asset Service Act, passed at third reading 2026-06-30; commencement set separately by the Executive Yuan, pending nine items of subsidiary legislation (FSC timetable: earliest Q1 2027). Until commencement only the AML registration regime (amended Money Laundering Control Act, operative 2024-11-30) is in force -- registration is not issuance authorization.
token_regime.note: Enactment is not commencement: no exportable authorizable token exists, so origin drag still fires on all TW-> edges whose destination does not already foreclose them.

inbound_gate.instrument: Virtual Asset Service Act: issuance and the inbound treatment of offshore stablecoins are conditioned on approval by the FSC AND the Central Bank of the Republic of China (Taiwan) -- dual approval, not FSC-with-CBC-consulted. Issuer-paid yield is prohibited. Roughly a 21-month operator transition.
inbound_gate.note: Anchored by verifier gate CF1 (TW = `T`). No gazetted commencement date, so the trigger is contingent and moves no horizon.

record_ref.note: `tw-frs-monetary_sovereignty-001` still cites the 'draft Virtual Asset Service Act'. The Act was passed at third reading on 2026-06-30 and is enacted, not draft. The signal is correct and the record's source prose is stale — the node-record face of finding F-TW. Recorded here rather than silently corrected: a legal record is edited by a verification pass, not by a build script.

egress_override.instrument: Atlas v0.2.5 carries no origin-override on the TW-> rows.

record_ref_gap.reason: An absence of an export/egress restriction is not an instrument and has no locator. The register carries no record asserting the absence; the Atlas's silence on the origin's row is the basis. Citation firewall (Citable by Construction §4.3): a null is honest where a plausible-looking locator would not be.
tier2_operational: a market fact carries no legal locator (SIG1/SG3).

token_regime.instrument: Virtual Asset User Protection Act (in force 2024-07-19) is a user-protection and market-abuse statute, not an issuance-licensing framework; domestic won-stablecoin issuance is effectively prohibited. The Digital Asset Basic Act remains an un-enacted bill (National Assembly Political Affairs Committee subcommittee).

record_ref.note: Vocabulary divergence: the register codes the draft Digital Asset Basic Act as `pending_proposal` (there IS a bill); the signal codes the absence of an operative issuance regime as `no_regime` (there is nothing in force to authorize under). Same instruments, different question asked of them.
record_ref.divergence: Vocabulary divergence: the register codes the draft Digital Asset Basic Act as `pending_proposal` (there IS a bill); the signal codes the absence of an operative issuance regime as `no_regime` (there is nothing in force to authorize under). Same instruments, different question asked of them.

inbound_gate.instrument: No operative inbound issuance-authorization regime to receive into (draft DABA).
inbound_gate.note: Under destination-first this makes ALL 11 inbound-KR edges `pre_regime` -- including CN->KR and TW->KR, which read `III` through v0.9.99 (NF-1).

record_ref.note: As KR.token_regime: `pending_proposal` (the bill exists) vs `no_regime` (nothing operative).
record_ref.divergence: As KR.token_regime: `pending_proposal` (the bill exists) vs `no_regime` (nothing operative).

egress_override.instrument: Korean foreign-exchange controls; Atlas v0.2.5 carries '[conditional origin-egress override (Korea FX, dormant)]' on all 11 KR-> rows -- conditional and dormant because there is no exportable won token for them to bite on.

record_ref.note: `kr-frs-monetary_sovereignty-001` cites the Foreign Exchange Transactions Act — the instrument the conditional, dormant Korea-FX override rests on.
record_ref.divergence: `kr-frs-monetary_sovereignty-001` cites the Foreign Exchange Transactions Act — the instrument the conditional, dormant Korea-FX override rests on.

yield_line.instrument: The leading DABA draft bars interest, discount or other reward to holders.
yield_line.note: A DRAFT provision -- cited in Feasibility s.4.5 as supporting evidence for the yield-line convergence, never as binding law.

record_ref_gap.reason: tier2_operational: a market fact carries no legal locator (SIG1/SG3).

settlement_blocs.note: Tier-2 operational enrichment, NOT a proposition of law (Feasibility s.5.1 concedes this explicitly). Drives infrastructure_overlap.bloc (verifier IB1) and NEVER class_code.

caveats.SG_ensemble: SG's FORMAL Project Ensemble membership is unconfirmed (VB-02); the HKMA-Ensemble / MAS-Guardian tie may be an alignment, not a shared substrate.
caveats.agora_is_not_G7: Project Agorá's seven central banks include the Bank of Korea and the Bank of Mexico; 'seven central banks convened by the BIS around major reserve currencies' is accurate, 'the G7-aligned bloc' is not (errata E7).
caveats.mbridge_is_not_PRC_and_Gulf: Post-BIS-withdrawal (2024-10-31) mBridge continues with China, Hong Kong, Thailand, the UAE and Saudi Arabia. Thailand is neither PRC nor Gulf (errata E7).
caveats.KR_tension: Korea is simultaneously an Agorá member and the register's sole pre-regime origin -- the settlement-bloc axis and the regulatory-maturity axis are not co-linear. A finding, not a defect.
